note: TB Mega Menu builds multi-column mega menus on top of Drupal core menus. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) and probe tb_megamenu-specific facts drawn only from source: the tb_megamenu config entity keyed {menu}__{theme}, the entity.tb_megamenu.collection admin route, the derivative tb_megamenu_menu_block block plugin, the block_config animation/style option values, JSON-string storage of block_config/menu_config, and the tb_megamenu.menu_builder service. No site mutation, no drush.

evals.prompt: In Drupal 11 with TB Mega Menu (tb_megamenu) 1.x, what kind of Drupal object stores a single mega menu's layout, and how is its ID formed? Answer briefly.
evals.expected_output: Each mega menu is a `tb_megamenu` config entity (config prefix menu_config, i.e. tb_megamenu.menu_config.{id}) whose id is {menu}__{theme}, e.g. main__olivero, pairing one core menu with one theme.

evals.prompt: Where in the Drupal admin do I create and manage TB Mega Menu menus, and what is the configure route name?
evals.expected_output: At Structure -> TB Mega Menu (/admin/structure/tb-megamenu), route entity.tb_megamenu.collection; add/edit/delete forms hang off that path. All gated by the 'administer tb_megamenu' permission.

evals.prompt: How do I display a TB Mega Menu on the site once it is configured? Name the block plugin involved and how there is one per menu.
evals.expected_output: Place a block: the tb_megamenu_menu_block block plugin uses a deriver (Plugin\Derivative\TBMegaMenuBlock) to expose one derivative block per configured menu (in the 'TB Mega Menu' category); add it to a region via Block layout.

evals.prompt: In TB Mega Menu 1.x, what animation effects can a mega menu block use, and what are the default animation and default color style?
evals.expected_output: Animation options are none, fading, slide, zoom, elastic (default 'none'); color style options are Default(''), black, blue, green (default Default). Duration defaults to 400ms and delay to 200ms.

evals.prompt: In the tb_megamenu config entity, how are the block_config and menu_config values stored, and what should I use to read them safely?
evals.expected_output: block_config and menu_config are stored as JSON-encoded strings on the config entity. Read them via the entity's getBlockConfig()/getMenuConfig() (which decode and HTML-escape item classes/captions/xicons/labels for XSS safety) and write via setBlockConfig()/setMenuConfig().

evals.prompt: Which service in TB Mega Menu would I call to programmatically load a menu's decoded configuration or render its block, and what is its id?
evals.expected_output: The tb_megamenu.menu_builder service (Drupal\tb_megamenu\TBMegaMenuBuilder, interface TBMegaMenuBuilderInterface); e.g. getMenus(), getMenuConfig($menu,$theme), getBlockConfig(), renderBlock(), getAllBlocks().

evals.prompt: Does TB Mega Menu replace Drupal core menus, and what happens when I edit the underlying menu in core? Answer briefly.
evals.expected_output: It does not replace core menus: a mega menu is built on top of an existing core menu, reading the core menu tree and merging its stored per-item config, so edits made in core's menu UI stay synchronized into the mega menu.
